name: Just Anomalies
short_description: A slow-burning psychological horror where the fear comes not from monsters, but from the unknown. Each step leads you back to the same room—familiar, yet subtly wrong. The silence weighs heavy, the air feels off, and the deeper you stare, the stronger the sense that something is staring back.
detailed_description:
"The room is waiting. It has always been waiting. But something is changing."

You open the door, step through—and find yourself back in the same room... Familiar, yet wrong in a way you can’t quite place. There’s a door behind you and a door ahead. Moving forward seems simple enough. Until it isn’t.

Every time you step through, the room reshapes itself, subtly… or not so subtly. A chair that wasn’t there before. A shadow that lingers a little too long. A feeling, creeping at the edges of your mind—that you’re being watched. But by what? Or by whom?

The rules are simple:

  • If the room is the same as when you first saw it, turn back.

  • If something has changed, move forward.

  • Count each right choice till the end.

There are no monsters. No chases. No jump scares. Just the growing weight of the unknown. The deeper you stare into the shifting reality, the stronger the feeling becomes—something is staring back.

Will you reach the end? Or will the room consume you first?

An eerie, slow-burning psychological horror where the only enemy is doubt.
